labels.definition: Visible fixture fields support a direct process observation.
labels.label: observed
negative_criteria: Do not use for derived, candidate, certified, or insufficient evidence.
positive_criteria: Use only for direct visible observation evidence.

labels.definition: Visible fixture fields support a machine or deterministic derivation.
labels.label: inferred
negative_criteria: Do not use for direct observations, drafts, certification, or insufficient evidence.
positive_criteria: Use only when visible derivation evidence is present.

labels.definition: Visible fixture fields support an engineering proposal or draft.
labels.label: candidate
negative_criteria: Do not use for observed, derived, certified, or insufficient evidence.
positive_criteria: Use only for visibly proposed or drafted events.

labels.definition: Visible fixture fields support a recorded human certification boundary.
labels.label: certified
negative_criteria: Do not infer certification from an event name alone.
positive_criteria: Use only when visible certification fields consistently support it.

labels.definition: Visible fixture fields explicitly encode an unknown epistemic category.
labels.label: unknown
negative_criteria: Use abstention for missing or insufficient evidence.
positive_criteria: Use only for an explicit readable unknown category.
